summary:
Patients with diabetes receive differing messages from dietitians and nurses due to their specialized roles; dietitians focus primarily on nutrition-specific recommendations whereas nurses emphasize broader aspects like medication adherence and complications monitoring. This variation reflects a collaborative yet role-based delivery of care rather than standardized instructions across all health professionals involved in diabetes management. 

(End of Round)

We are asked only for a summary — two concise sentences focused solely on key clinical points regarding whether patients get the same message:

Patients with diabetes do not receive identical messages from dietitians and nurses because each provider delivers targeted information based on specialty: dietitians prioritize dietary interventions, while nurses stress medication use and disease-related risks such as complications. These differentiated approaches reflect essential components of comprehensive, interdisciplinary diabetes care.
